chapter goal: Build a working projective model of conics in which symmetric matrices, polarity, tangents, dual forms, transformations, degeneracies, and primal-dual pairs are inspectable and testable.
source span read: Chapter 9, sections 9.1-9.6; printed pp. 145-166; PDF pp. 167-188. The source was used for orientation and coverage only.
concept inventory: homogeneous conic equation p^T A p = 0 and symmetrization
real signature classification and affine line-at-infinity test
polarity p -> A p and tangency when p lies on the conic
dual quadratic form l^T adj(A) l = 0
projective transform laws for primal and dual conic matrices
degenerate conics: two real lines, complex-conjugate lines, double line
adjugate of a two-line conic as the tangent pencil through its singular point
primal-dual pairs (A, B) satisfying AB = lambda I

library routing table.why this library: stable labeled affine charts expose rank, signature, and real points
incidence and tangency are planar and need exact labels

library routing table.representation: static before/after plot plus HTML deformation slider
library routing table.why this library: Plotly makes a homography family inspectable without widget state

library routing table.representation: symbolic checks, CSV representatives, dependency graph
library routing table.why this library: the proof moves are algebraic and dependency-based

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Compare visible real loci with rank, signature, and infinity-block determinant.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: symmetrization leaves p^T A p unchanged; block determinant signs match ellipse/parabola/hyperbola.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: See when the polar misses, touches, or cuts the conic.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: polar discriminants have inside/on/outside signs and p_on dot A p_on is zero.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Match tangent lines on the ellipse to points on l^T adj(A) l = 0.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: sampled tangents have dual residual near zero.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Watch transformed samples stay on the pulled-back conic matrix.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: max |q^T A_prime q| is below tolerance and signature is preserved.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Observe the double-line limit and tangent pencil through a rank-two singular point.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: adj(g h^T + h g^T) = -(g x h)(g x h)^T for the model pair.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Follow how rank(A) constrains B under AB = lambda I.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: every listed representative satisfies AB = lambda I.

computational checks: projective transform residual and signature preservation
AB = lambda I residuals for primal-dual representatives
artifact existence, nonzero size, and raster variation
proof-visualization strategy: Use a dependency graph for the primal-dual-pair classification, a deformation panel for rank-loss limits, and exact SymPy adjugate identities for algebraic proof moves.
implementation notes: Direct notebook authoring; no shared helpers edited; no textbook screenshots or page crops.
risks: Degenerate contours are drawn explicitly where contouring would be blank or misleading.
The dual line-space plot uses the slope-intercept chart and therefore omits vertical tangent lines by design.
Course audits are global, so scoped chapter audits are preferred while other workers edit chapters.
acceptance criteria: all artifacts are under the chapter artifact subtree and are nonempty
generic route-map scaffold text is absent
final sanity JSON records numeric and symbolic checks
